creator: mark.picone@deakin.edu.au
text:
Mark Picone wrote:
> Wesley,
>
> I have done further testing and it appears this was only a local issue 
> after all (over several boxes).
>
> You can now close the PR.
>
> Thanks & Regards
> Mark Picone
>
> Mark Picone wrote:
>>
>>
>> ---------- Forwarded message ----------
>> From: *Wesley Shields* <wxs@freebsd.org <mailto:wxs@freebsd.org>>
>> Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2008 at 1:46 AM
>> Subject: Re: ports/120831: security/tripwire: Tripwire fails to make 
>> keys when compiled with -O2 (default) from ports]
>> To: Mark Picone <markjpicone@gmail.com <mailto:markjpicone@gmail.com>>
>> Cc: bug-followup@freebsd.org <mailto:bug-followup@freebsd.org>
>>
>>
>> On Sat, Mar 01, 2008 at 10:54:06AM +1100, Mark Picone wrote:
>> > mx1.freebsd.org <http://mx1.freebsd.org> makes my emails disappear 
>> sometimes.
>> >
>> > ---------- Forwarded message ----------
>> > From: Mark Picone <mark@markpicone.com.au 
>> <mailto:mark@markpicone.com.au>>
>> > Date: Sat, Mar 1, 2008 at 10:52 AM
>> > Subject: [Fwd: Re: ports/120831: security/tripwire: Tripwire fails 
>> to make
>> > keys when compiled with -O2 (default) from ports]
>> > To: markjpicone@gmail.com <mailto:markjpicone@gmail.com>
>> >
>> > I have only had this problem on i386 hardware. This worked fine on 
>> SPARC64
>> > with -O2 and AMD64 now as well it seems.
>> >
>> > "make install clean" is all that needs to be done, it gets stuck on key
>> > generation which is done as part of the install process as far as i 
>> know.
>> >
>> > - Mark Picone
>>
>> I just tried this on a 6.2 VM (i386) and was unable to reproduce it.  Is
>> there any chance you can get a ktrace of the process so I can see what
>> it's doing?
>>
>> -- WXS
>>
>
